source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage/Crane - Noble Don Taylor

event.top_event: Worker back injury from overextension while removing cable clamps
actions_taken: Injured person safely operated work basket to receive assistance
Coworkers helped remove injured person from work basket
Injured person transported to rig hospital for treatment
Helicopter evacuation to West Isle Urgent Care in Galveston, TX on 2/8/2016
Medical evaluation including x-rays and MRI
Injured person assigned to office duties with restrictions
event.summary: Worker was removing MUX cable clamps from a work basket over the moon pool using an impact wrench. While reaching to remove a cable clamp, the worker heard a popping sound and experienced severe lower back pain and numbness in right leg. The work basket was unable to fully reach the work area, requiring the worker to overextend. Worker was evacuated via helicopter and hospitalized for three days. Despite medical clearance for full duty, the worker could only perform 50-60% of normal duties and was reassigned to office work with restrictions.
key_phrases: work basket unable to fully reach work area
overextension while removing MUX cable clamps

hazards.name: Impact wrench operation during extended reach

threats.name: Worker overextension while removing cable clamps
Inadequate work basket reach to work area
Impact wrench use during body extension

consequences.name: Worker back injury with nerve involvement
Reduced worker capability and restricted duty assignment

supporting_text: Work basket is unable to fully reach the work area, which requires the worker to overextend while removing the MUX cable clamps.

controls.name: Work procedures for cable clamp removal
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to prevent unsafe work practices

supporting_text: Working with an impact wrench while body is extended.

controls.name: Supervisor oversight and job safety assessment
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to identify and prevent unsafe work conditions

supporting_text: Crew members were in the process of pulling riser after the completion of the well. Injured Person (IP) was working from a work basket over the moon pool.

controls.name: Medical evaluation and fitness for duty assessment
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to prevent return to work of unfit personnel

supporting_text: IP received treatment and care from the medic from 18:15 on 2/6/2016 until approximately 08:30 on 2/8/16 when he was evacuated via helicopter
IP was prescribed narcotics, and the doctor ordered x-rays and an MRI
the doctor released him for full work duty without any restrictions on 2/11/16

BSEE Investigators asked if the IP felt he could carry out his job responsibilities as a Floor Man at 100 percent. The IP responded by saying he could probably only conduct 50-60 percent of his duties at best

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
